title: Artanthe eximia
intro: Artanthe eximia, also known as the "Heart-Leaf", is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Artanthe eximia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Artanthe eximia is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in infusions and salves to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Artanthe eximia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. This family consists of approximately 800 species of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Commelinaceae family is part of the order Commelinales and the clade monocots. Monocots are a group of flowering plants that have one cotyledon, or seed leaf, in their seeds. The classification of Artanthe eximia is further refined through its placement in the subfamily Commelinoideae.
